games.name: Snake
games.description: Navigate the serpent through a digital grid. Consume pixels to grow, but one wrong move means total annihilation.

comments: Four separate sound functions for a snake game. One eats food, one dies. That's... two events. You used four.
A `lerpColor` function with `hexToRGB` just to smoothly color snake body segments. Picasso painted apples too.
The color palette object `C` has 13 named colors including `borderGlow`. Snake has a glow border. Sure.
`easeOutCubic` interpolation for snake movement. The snake moves in discrete grid steps. ON A GRID.
Section headers using ═══ unicode box-drawing characters. It's a snake game, not a NASA control panel.
`playDirectionSound` creates white noise via a buffer. A tick sound. In a snake game. Meticulously engineered.
Uses `Math.min(1, tickAcc / tickRate)` for sub-tick interpolation. The snake moves 10 pixels at a time.
`roundRect` utility function defined from scratch. canvas.roundRect exists natively. Classic Opus.
The mute button has THREE separate event listeners: pointerdown, pointerup, AND click. For a mute button.
It's wrapped in an IIFE with `'use strict'`. The snake eats dots strictly and within proper scope.
851 lines and still flexing initAudio, playEatSound, and shakeTime like Snake got a soundtrack budget.
resumeAudio on click, pointerdown, keydown? Opus built three wake-up calls for one hungry noodle.
placeFood, tick, die, emitParticles—this snake runs a whole production pipeline, not a toy.
The palette jumps from #00ff88 to #ff0066 like a neon nightclub swallowed a grid.
drawBackground, drawGrid, drawHUD, drawGameOverScreen—rival snakes barely draw shame.
lastLevel plus playLevelUpSound means even your score spikes get a victory lap.
That muteBtn flips icons; competitor UX usually stops at hopes and prayers.
